QF.id: I Codes of Conduct and Investor Protection_31

question.text: If initiation of a conciliation process takes place after six months from the date of the transaction or dispute, what fee must the initiator pay?

answer.text: Rs. 1,000 late fee, collected by the MII
Rs. 5,000 late fee paid to the arbitrator
No late fee — the six-month rule only applies to arbitration

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The text states: "Initiation of conciliation process after six months from the date of transaction/dispute arising will require payment of Rs 1000/- by the initiator of the complaint/dispute (whether such initiator be the investor/client or the Market Participant) and shall be collected by the MIIs." **The Trap:** Doubling fees applies specifically to late arbitration (after one month from failed conciliation and up to six months). The conciliation late fee is a flat Rs. 1,000 — simple and fixed. **Cross-Exam Context:** Late fees apply to both conciliation (after 6 months, flat Rs. 1,000) and arbitration (after 1 month from failed conciliation, double the non-refundable fees; after 6 months by MP, additional 50% per month). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Conciliation late fee = Rs. 1,000 flat after 6 months. Arbitration late fee = double if initiated after 1 month from conciliation failure.
